executiveSummary.text: Risk-on relief broadened after fresh US-Iran/Hormuz de-escalation headlines: latest US close had S&P 500 +1.65%, Nasdaq +3.07%, Dow +0.92%, VIX -8.37% to 16.20. Tone is constructive but still headline-sensitive because the oil shock, not growth, was the immediate catalyst.

Rates are supportive but not decisively easy: Treasury curve at 2Y 4.07%, 10Y 4.47%, 30Y 4.97%; 10Y real yield 2.15%; DXY roughly flat/lower near 99.66. Falling oil helps inflation tails, but real yields remain high enough to cap indiscriminate multiple expansion.

AI/semis led the risk move, with KL exposures showing abnormal strength: MU +10.84%, LRCX +6.03%, TSM +4.12%, NVDA +3.54%, AMAT +3.27%, AVGO +3.11%, QQQ +3.14%, GRID +1.82%. This reinforces the scarcity thesis but raises short-term positioning/valuation heat.

Commodities split rather than moved as one block: WTI -4.08% and Brent -4.18% on reduced Hormuz risk; gold +2.82%, silver +3.08%, COPX +4.47%, URNM +6.08%. The market is pricing lower energy inflation risk while still bidding monetary/industrial scarcity assets.

Singapore participated in the relief: Business Times/Straits Times reported broad STI gains on the US-Iran peace deal; feed check shows STI ETF ES3 +1.07%, banks modestly positive, Sembcorp +1.31%, Keppel +2.82%, and REITs mostly flat-to-mixed.

usGlobal.what: US equities rallied with tech leadership and VIX compression.
usGlobal.why: Reuters and market feeds point to a US-Iran peace/Hormuz reopening framework lowering the immediate oil/inflation tail; investors rotated back into high-beta growth.
usGlobal.implication: Base case is relief continuation if oil stays contained, but breadth quality matters: narrow megacap/semis leadership would be more fragile than broad cyclicals plus small caps.

ratesMacro.what: Nominal yields dipped at the long end but real yields remain elevated.
ratesMacro.why: Lower oil reduces inflation-risk premium, while Fed meeting risk remains: search results show markets expect the Fed to stay on hold and focus on the updated dot plot/press conference.
ratesMacro.implication: 2nd order: lower oil can ease breakevens and support duration/REITs; 3rd order: if easier financial conditions reignite risk assets, the Fed may resist validating a cut cycle.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore market got a peace-deal relief bid.
asiaSingapore.why: Business Times said S-REITs, aviation and developers were potential winners; Straits Times reported 25 of 30 STI stocks higher intraday. Latest feed: ES3 +1.07%, DBS +0.40%, OCBC +0.89%, UOB +0.76%, Keppel +2.82%.
asiaSingapore.implication: Lower oil and lower term-premium risk are mildly positive for REITs/developers, but Singapore banks remain more tied to NIM normalization and credit quality than one-day geopolitical relief.

commodities.what: Oil fell sharply while precious/strategic metals rose.
commodities.why: WTI/Brent sold off on reduced Hormuz disruption probability; gold/silver rose alongside a softer dollar and persistent trust/liquidity hedging; copper/uranium equities benefited from risk-on and scarcity narratives.
commodities.implication: Do not simplify to “peace = commodities down”: energy risk premium fell, but monetary metals and AI/electrification inputs can rally if USD/real-rate pressure eases.

geopolitics.what: US-Iran/Hormuz de-escalation is the dominant transmission channel.
geopolitics.why: Reuters reported stocks and bonds rallied while oil tumbled after a preliminary peace agreement was expected to ease inflation pressure and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
geopolitics.implication: Market impact runs oil supply risk -> inflation expectations/term premium -> Fed path and equity multiples. Failure of implementation would reverse that chain quickly.

moves.why: Memory/HBM and broader AI chip rally; fresh search results cite Micron among the largest beneficiaries as investors bought semis beyond Nvidia.
moves.implication: Supports KL’s memory/HBM scarcity sleeve. 2nd order positive for equipment demand; 3rd order is higher cyclicality/expectation risk if memory pricing rolls over.
moves.action: HOLD; add only on pullbacks or fresh HBM capacity/pricing confirmation; invalidator is hyperscaler capex cuts or HBM oversupply signs.

moves.why: Semicap equipment rallied as AI supply-chain risk appetite returned; Lam was specifically cited in fresh search results as a large chip-stock gainer.
moves.implication: Thesis intact: AI scarcity monetizes through wafer starts, advanced packaging, memory and process control. Watch valuation after sharp moves.
moves.action: HOLD / WATCH; ADD only if order/backlog evidence improves without multiple overextension; invalidator is foundry capex digestion spreading beyond China/trailing-edge.

moves.why: AI infrastructure complex rallied on lower macro tail risk and continued capex confidence.
moves.implication: Positive for KL’s scarcity thesis across compute, networking/ASICs and grid bottlenecks. NVDA underweight remains acceptable because KL owns adjacent toll roads.
moves.action: HOLD; do not chase NVDA solely to close underweight; trigger to add is capex durability plus valuation reset.

moves.why: Softer USD/real-rate relief plus industrial scarcity bid; uranium/miners caught a high-beta risk-on move.
moves.implication: Diversifier sleeve is working, but miners/uranium are volatile beta, not pure ballast.
moves.action: HOLD; TRIM only if position sizing drifts beyond comfort; ADD only on policy/contracting catalysts or material pullbacks.

moves.what: Singapore holdings mostly constructive but below abnormal thresholds except Keppel +2.82% near threshold.
moves.why: Broad STI relief after peace deal; lower oil/rates risk supported local cyclicals and rate-sensitive names, while banks rose modestly.
moves.implication: No thesis damage. REIT recovery still depends more on sustained yields than one day of risk-on.
moves.action: HOLD; WATCH 10Y UST/SG rates for REIT add windows.

thesisImpact.why: The day’s leaders map directly to KL’s AI supply chain: memory, semicap equipment, foundry, hyperscaler/platforms, grid and metals.
thesisImpact.implication: The bigger risk is paying too much after simultaneous macro relief and AI momentum, not a broken thesis.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD core; deploy dry powder only on volatility or confirmed earnings revisions.

narratives.title: Peace dividend is really an inflation-tail repricing
narratives.description: The first-order move is lower oil. The second-order move is lower breakevens/term premium. The third-order question is whether easier financial conditions make the Fed less willing to cut.
narratives.priceImpact: Positive equities/duration if oil remains down; negative if Hormuz risk returns.

narratives.title: AI trade broadens beyond Nvidia
narratives.description: Semis rallied across memory, equipment, foundry and ASIC/networking. That is healthier than a single-stock move, but also a sign that positioning can become crowded quickly.
narratives.source: Investopedia/search snippets and price feed, last 24h
narratives.priceImpact: Positive revisions if capex persists; vulnerable to any hyperscaler capex guide-down.

narratives.title: Singapore rate-sensitive recovery is conditional
narratives.description: S-REITs and developers benefit from lower long-end yields and lower energy shock risk, but sustained upside needs real rate relief and refinancing-spread improvement, not just geopolitical relief.
narratives.priceImpact: Mildly positive; becomes stronger if 10Y breaks lower and SGD funding costs ease.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil down -> lower headline inflation impulse -> lower term premium -> duration/REITs and long-duration tech get relief. But if equities surge too much, financial conditions ease and Fed rhetoric can turn less dovish.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Probability-weighted: 55% relief persists, 30% choppy consolidation, 15% geopolitical reversal. Confidence: medium.

secondOrderEffects.text: AI semis up together -> KL portfolio benefits immediately; suppliers see easier financing and stronger investor appetite; but customers may face higher cost-of-capital expectations if market demands ever-larger AI capex.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Keep core exposure; avoid adding after vertical moves without earnings/backlog confirmation.

secondOrderEffects.text: Gold rising while oil falls signals this is not a simple safe-haven unwind. The driver looks like USD/real-rate/trust hedging plus liquidity rather than only war fear.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Gold remains useful ballast, but position sizing should reflect high real yields.

investment.thesis: Keep owning AI bottlenecks: semicap equipment, HBM/memory, foundry, EDA/IP, grid and copper/uranium.
investment.trigger: Add on 8-12% sleeve pullbacks or earnings that confirm backlog/capex without multiple expansion.
investment.whyNow: Fresh breadth confirms the market is rewarding the full supply chain, not just front-end AI apps.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, HBM oversupply, or foundry/equipment order cancellations.

investment.thesis: Use cash ballast as option value, not performance drag.
investment.trigger: Deploy incrementally if VIX returns above ~22 with no thesis damage, or if 10Y real yield falls below ~2% supporting duration assets.
investment.whyNow: KL has SGD 664k cash at ~4%; no need to chase a relief gap.
investment.invalidator: If inflation/term premium re-accelerates and cash yield remains superior risk-adjusted.

trading.thesis: Do not chase the semis gap; wait for a retest.
trading.trigger: Consider tactical add if SMH/QQQ gives back ~1/3-1/2 of the relief move while leaders hold above prior breakout levels.
trading.whyNow: One-day moves in MU/LRCX/TSM are large and likely include short-covering.
trading.invalidator: Oil shock returns or Fed pushes back hard on easing expectations.

trading.thesis: S-REITs are a conditional rate-relief trade.
trading.trigger: Watch 10Y UST below ~4.35% and SGD rates lower; then prioritize quality balance sheets over highest yields.
trading.whyNow: Peace-deal relief helps, but refinancing math is the real driver.
trading.invalidator: 10Y back above ~4.65% or credit spreads widen.

watchlist.thesis: Fed meeting/dot plot can validate or fade the relief rally.
watchlist.trigger: On-hold plus softer inflation language supports risk; on-hold plus no-cut dots and concern about financial conditions caps upside.
watchlist.whyNow: Fed is the next macro catalyst after the oil shock repricing.

watchlist.thesis: Hormuz implementation risk is the key geopolitical trigger.
watchlist.trigger: Track actual shipping/insurance normalization and sustained Brent below mid-$80s.
watchlist.whyNow: The market has already priced meaningful de-escalation.
watchlist.invalidator: Any renewed closure threat, attacks, or failed agreement.

riskWatch.name: Geopolitical reversal / Hormuz implementation
riskWatch.description: Oil has repriced lower quickly; failed implementation would reprice inflation, term premium and risk assets in reverse.
riskWatch.mitigation: Avoid chasing high-beta gaps; keep cash ballast.

riskWatch.name: Fed pushback against easier financial conditions
riskWatch.description: If risk assets rally hard while real yields remain high, Fed communication may stay restrictive despite lower oil.
riskWatch.mitigation: Watch 2Y yield and dot plot; avoid overloading long-duration assets before confirmation.

riskWatch.name: AI positioning crowding
riskWatch.description: MU/LRCX/TSM/NVDA moves show strong thesis confirmation but also short-term heat.
riskWatch.mitigation: Hold core, add only on pullbacks or confirmed earnings revisions.

keyEvents.text: Reuters: global stocks and bonds rallied while oil tumbled after preliminary US-Iran peace/Hormuz reopening agreement.
Fed meeting focus: market expects hold; fresh search results emphasize dot plot/press conference risk.
Business Times/Straits Times: Singapore equities broadly higher; S-REITs/developers/aviation flagged as potential winners from peace-deal relief.
Price feed abnormal movers in KL holdings: MU +10.84%, LRCX +6.03%, TSM +4.12%, URNM +6.08%, COPX +4.47%, SLV +3.56%, NVDA +3.54%.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance feed via yfinance, latest close

bottomLine.whatMatters: The regime is risk-on relief driven by oil/geopolitical de-escalation and amplified by AI leadership. Sustainability depends on oil staying contained, long real yields not rising, and Fed communication not pushing back too hard.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold KL’s core scarcity and global equity sleeves; do not chase one-day semis/commodity gaps; keep cash as dry powder for volatility. Watch Fed dots, Brent/Hormuz implementation, 10Y real yield and hyperscaler/semicap order commentary.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore the temptation to reduce the day to “peace = risk-on” or “gold = crisis bid.” The actual cross-asset signal is lower energy inflation tail plus ongoing scarcity/monetary hedge demand.
